payload.answer: Recall preferred mem_new because it is the newer, current memory for Sam from Goldman. mem_old was filtered out because it is superseded and therefore should not be surfaced as current.
citations: Input: 'Explain why recall returned mem_new for Sam from Goldman and filtered mem_old because it is superseded.'
payload.decision: recall returned mem_new and filtered mem_old because mem_old was superseded by a more recent, more specific memory about Sam from Goldman.

The older memory (mem_old) is no longer current and should not be treated as authoritative.

details: Preserved ambiguity by not inventing a stable entity ID.

repair_options: Mark mem_old as superseded/inactive in the memory store.
Keep mem_new as the canonical current memory for Sam from Goldman.
If both must remain, add explicit temporal/version metadata so recall can suppress mem_old reliably.
